About.Name: Daiki Hagiwara
About.AboutText: Hi, I am Daiki Hagiwara. I am a fourth-year Computer Science (BS) student from Japan at Seattle Pacific University. I am passionate about developing applications and games, and I am always eager to learn new things, which has helped me build strong technical skills across multiple areas of software development.

Experience.ExperienceText: I am a Computer Science student at Seattle Pacific University with a strong passion for software development, systems design, and building practical technology solutions. My journey began as an international student, where I developed communication, leadership, and adaptability skills while working with diverse communities. Currently, I focus on programming, problem-solving, and creating projects that combine technical ability with real-world impact.
Experience.EducationText: Pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science with coursework in software engineering, systems design, algorithms, networking, and web development.
Experience.SkillsText: Experienced in object-oriented programming, web development, cloud tools, and building full-stack projects. Strong interest in scalable systems, security, and modern software engineering practices.

Project1.Title: Security Camera
Project1.Desc:
Built a real-time security camera system using Raspberry Pi.

Streams live video to remote viewers through WebRTC.

Designed for low-latency peer-to-peer communication without cloud dependency.

Project2.Title: Spotify Playlist Automation
Project2.Desc:
Developed a serverless Spotify playlist automation system using AWS Lambda.

Designed the application to retrieve top tracks and top artists for multiple users and update playlists automatically.

Implemented persistent state management with Amazon S3 for user metadata, playlist information, and Spotify OAuth cache files.

Enabled scheduled execution through AWS EventBridge to support fully automated periodic updates.

Project3.Title: Reverse Engineering & API Analysis
Project3.Desc:
Analyzed real-world platforms (Instagram, Spotify, YouTube) through unofficial APIs.

Reverse engineered request/response structures and authentication flows.

Explored how modern applications communicate securely over networks.

Focused on understanding system design, not bypassing restrictions.

Built custom Python applications using reconstructed unofficial APIs.

Designed workflows to transform unstructured API responses into meaningful insights.
